title: Number Of Beautiful Partitions
description: Count partitions of a digit string into k pieces of length at least minLength, each starting with a prime digit and ending with a non-prime digit, modulo 1e9+7. Inputs use Python lists and integers; unless explicitly stated, callers need not preserve mutable inputs.
input_contract: Parameters in order: s: str, k: int, minLength: int. 1 <= len(s) <= 1000; s contains digits; 1 <= k,minLength <= len(s); answer modulo 1000000007.
output_contract: Return integer using exact comparison. Python integer arithmetic has no 32-bit overflow; array/query order is significant.
